what_it_does: Defines browser-side Vi helpers for the ConceptV Type Registry that ask window.claude to draft Type schemas, promote instances into Types, and suggest slots, then expose those helpers on window.CV_TYPES_VI.
observations: Runs inside an IIFE and exits if window.CV_REGISTRY is missing.
Uses a fixed layer taxonomy string injected into every prompt.
Parses loose JSON by trying the whole reply first, then an object-shaped substring.
proposeType builds a prompt from a user brief, optional parent Type context, optional locked layer/family hints, and a compact catalogue of up to 80 registered non-template Types.
promoteInstance clones an instance, removes id and createdAt, asks Vi to extract reusable Type metadata, and stores the slim instance under defaults.
suggestSlots asks Vi for 2-4 additional slot definitions for an existing Type and returns an empty array on missing type or failure.
Returned Type drafts are normalized with generated ids, fallback descriptions, tags, variables, defaults, icons, and provenance set to vi.
standouts: The file turns LLM responses into registry-shaped draft Type metadata.
Prompt text embeds ConceptV layer rules, brand voice, slot semantics, variable guidance, and JSON output contracts.
ID generation is local and registry-aware through R.get conflict checks.
conventions: Browser global module pattern: read window.CV_REGISTRY and window.claude, write window.CV_TYPES_VI.
Async helper functions construct prompts, call window.claude.complete, parse JSON, and normalize return shapes.
Type ids follow layer.family.kebab-name with numeric suffixes when needed.
Fallback values preserve expected object shapes when optional LLM fields are absent.
concerns: parseJsonLoose uses a greedy brace regex, so replies with extra brace-delimited text can produce an invalid or unintended parse target.
User brief, registry fields, and instance JSON are interpolated directly into prompts without escaping or prompt-boundary hardening.
JSON truncation with slice can cut defaults or instance data mid-structure in prompt context.
proposeType trusts layerHint or parsed.layer without local validation against the documented layer list.
notes: This file is the Vi-facing Type Registry drafting layer: it prompts for Type metadata, slot ideas, and instance promotion, then normalizes the responses for registry use.
questions: What exact shape does CV_REGISTRY.resolve return when inheritance chains are involved?
What icon names are valid in the CV icon library?
How does window.claude.complete report errors or rate limits?
Should generated layers and families be validated before callers persist the returned drafts?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implemented helpers: propose Type schemas from briefs, suggest slots, suggest defaults through prompt output, and promote instances into reusable Types.
apparent_themes: Reusable Type extraction from concrete instances
intention_signals: The file seems intended to let Vi act as a structured assistant for expanding the ConceptV Type Registry while keeping generated output compatible with local registry schemas and naming rules.
novelty: It combines design-system layer taxonomy, brand voice constraints, existing registry catalogue context, and instance-to-template variable extraction in prompt-driven browser helpers.
contribution: This file contributes the interactive interpretation and drafting functions that convert briefs or existing instances into reusable Type metadata, including ids, slots, variables, defaults, tags, icons, and provenance suitable for a fused registry workflow.
summary_for_embedding: Browser-side Vi helper module for ConceptV Type Registry that prompts an LLM to propose Types, promote instances, and suggest slots, then normalizes the returned JSON metadata.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to support assisted registry authoring by turning design briefs and existing ConceptV instances into structured reusable Type definitions.

symbols.does: Attempts to parse a string as JSON, falling back to a brace-delimited object substring.
symbols.description: This helper accepts only string replies, first tries JSON.parse on the full reply, then searches for an object-looking substring and tries to parse that. It returns null when neither path yields valid JSON.

symbols.does: Converts an input value into a lowercase kebab-case identifier fragment with a random fallback.
symbols.description: This helper stringifies the input, lowercases and trims it, replaces non-alphanumeric runs with hyphens, and strips leading or trailing hyphens. Empty results become a type-prefixed random suffix.

symbols.does: Builds a registry Type id from layer, optional family, and name, adding a numeric suffix on conflict.
symbols.description: This helper creates ids in the form layer.family.kebab-name or layer.kebab-name and checks R.get to avoid collisions. If the base id exists, it increments a -2, -3 style suffix until an unused id is found.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi to create a draft Type from a brief, optional parent Type, and optional layer or family hints.
symbols.description: This async helper gathers parent context when provided, lists up to 80 registered non-template Types for slot references, and asks window.claude.complete for a strict JSON Type proposal. It parses the reply, requires at least name and layer, then returns a normalized draft with id, layer, family, description, tags, extends, slots, defaults, variables, icon, and provenance.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi to turn an existing instance into reusable Type metadata with extracted variables.
symbols.description: This async helper clones the instance, removes id and createdAt, and sends truncated instance JSON to Vi with a target layer and family. It returns a Type-shaped object with generated id, metadata from the parsed response where available, the slim instance under defaults, extracted variables, and provenance set to vi.

symbols.does: Prompts Vi to suggest additional slot definitions for an existing Type.
symbols.description: This async helper resolves a Type by id, describes its current name, layer, family, description, and slots, then asks for 2-4 new slot suggestions. It parses the response and returns parsed slots when present, otherwise an empty array on missing Type or caught error.
